catalysts.catalyst_details: Atlas Revenue growth accelerated for the third consecutive quarter to +30% in F3Q26, up from +29% in F2Q26.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Atlas Revenue growth accelerated a third consecutive quarter to +30% (from +29% in F2Q26)

catalysts.catalyst_details: Non-Atlas revenue outperformed expectations in F3Q26, largely driven by higher multi-year deals.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Non-Atlas outperformed largely on higher multi-year deals

catalysts.catalyst_details: Operating Margin (OpM) and Free Cash Flow Margin (FCFM) came in +800 bps and +1800 bps ahead of expectations, respectively, in F3Q26.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: OpM / FCFM came in +800 bps / + 1800 bps ahead on Revenue outperformance and higher cash collections.

catalysts.catalyst_details: MongoDB delivered a second quarter in FY26 at a Rule of 40+, the highest since F1Q24, illustrating a transition to durable and profitable high-growth.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Importantly, MongoDB delivered a second quarter in FY26 at a Rule of 40+, highest since F1Q24.

catalysts.catalyst_details: MongoDB provided F4Q26 Revenue and Operating Income guidance +6.6% and +59% above Street expectations, respectively, including +27% Atlas growth guidance and high-single-digit Non-Atlas growth embedding multi-year deals.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: The +22% AH stock move reflects F3Q momentum plus F4Q Revenue and Op. Income guidance +6.6% / +59% above Street (incl. Atlas guidance +27% vs. Street +24%). We also recap F4Q26 Guidance: 1) +27% Atlas growth (vs. mid-20% prior), which still accounts for historical seasonal variability and consumption patterns during the holiday season, and 2) High-single-digit Non-Atlas growth that embeds several large multi-year deals for which Management has high visibility (closed or expected to close in 4Q).

catalysts.catalyst_details: MongoDB added +2500 new Atlas customers in F3Q26, stable compared to F2Q and well-above the +1800 baseline over the past three years, indicating a strengthening Product-Led Growth (PLG) motion.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: New logo activity stood out again with +2500 new Atlas customers (stable vs. F2Q) and well-above the +1800 baseline over the past three years, underscoring a strengthening PLG motion that could benefit further as MongoDB’s incremental Bay Area investments in developer awareness mature and MongoDB’s AI capabilities make their way to Community Edition.

catalysts.catalyst_details: The Version 8.0 release of the core engine delivered considerable performance gains, including 36% faster reads and 59% higher update throughput.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: lower-friction customer onboarding plus performance gains on the core engine (Version 8.0 release) can simultaneously attract new customer at a rapid clip, broadening the wallet share opportunity. In Version 8.0, MongoDB delivered considerable performance gains – 36% faster reads and 59% higher update throughput; we believe this matters as customers move larger and more business-critical systems onto Atlas.

catalysts.catalyst_details: MongoDB is positioning Atlas as the context engine for AI applications with natively integrated Vector Search and Voyage models, unlocking new consumption vectors across its 60K+ Atlas customers.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Ultimately, we see MongoDB emerging as the context engine for AI applications with natively integrated Vector Search plus Voyage models, unlocking new consumption vectors across its 60K+ Atlas customers on top of an already $100B+ core operational database market ripe for modernization

catalysts.catalyst_details: Net ARR Expansion Rate improved to 120% in F3Q26 from 119% in the prior quarter.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Note Net ARR Expansion Rate improved to 120% from 119% in the prior Q.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Gross Margin declined to 74% in F3Q26 (vs. 77% in F3Q25) as Atlas mix expanded.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: On margins: GM declined to 74% (vs. 77% in F3Q25) as Atlas mix expanded.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Planned Sales & Marketing (S&M) and Research & Development (R&D) investments that took longer to implement in F3Q26 are now shifting into F4Q26 and FY27.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: OpM came in +800 bps ahead on Revenue outperformance and lower-than-expected OpEx, as planned S&M and R&D investments took longer to implement and are now shifting into F4Q26 and FY27.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Management reaffirmed its framework to deliver 100-200 basis points of Operating Margin expansion in FY27 and beyond.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Despite continued investments in R&D and sales capacity, Management reaffirmed its framework to deliver +100-200 bps of OpM expansion in FY27 and beyond.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Management expects Free Cash Flow (FCF) conversion to be >100% in FY26 and continues to target 80-100% FCF conversion in FY27 and beyond.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Management now expects FCF conversion to be >100% in FY26 and continues to target 80-100% FCF conversion in FY27 and beyond as telegraphed at the Investor Day.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Key areas to monitor for future upside include the pace of new logo adds, incremental upside from multi-year deals, and durability of workload expansion within existing larger customer accounts.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: From here, we continue to watch: 1) The pace of new logo adds to validate MongoDB’s sustained relevance in the modern data stack, 2) Incremental upside from multi-year deals, as the updated guidance only contemplates deals where Management has clear visibility, and 3) The durability of workload expansion within existing accounts, particularly larger customers, underpinned by recent GTM investments.

catalysts.catalyst_details: The Atlas platform now spans full-text search, vector search, stream processing, data federation, time-series, and robust security (including queryable encryption), consolidating functionalities.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: The platform now spans full-text search, vector search, stream processing, data federation, time-series, and robust security (including queryable encryption), collapsing moving parts and speeding time-to-value.

catalysts.catalyst_details: The Database Management Systems market is large and expanding ($100B+ growing at +16% CAGR through CY29), with worldwide data generation expected to grow at +25% CAGR from CY25-29.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: The backdrop is attractive: the Database Management Systems market is large and expanding ($100B+ growing at +16% CAGR through CY29, Exhibit 1) and worldwide data generation is expected to grow at a +25% CAGR from CY25-29 (IDC, Exhibit 2).

catalysts.catalyst_details: Atlas revenue has compounded at over 60% from FY19-26E, reaching more than $1.8B in run-rate revenue, validating product-market fit.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Atlas has compounded at over 60% from FY19-26E to more than $1.8B in run-rate Revenue despite native cloud and open-source alternatives, validating product–market fit and customer trust.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Postgres has shown strong momentum, becoming the most-used database in the Stack Overflow Developer Survey (58% in 2025), indicating increased competitive pressure.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Postgres’ momentum is clear: it has become the most-used database in the Stack Overflow Developer Survey – 52% of Professional Developers reported using it in 2024 and 58% in 2025 (Exhibit 3), putting it well ahead of competing database technologies.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Snowflake's acquisition of Crunchy Data and Databricks' acquisition of Neon validate Postgres as a strategic anchor for transactional and AI workloads, intensifying competitive dynamics.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Snowflake’s purchase of Crunchy Data and Databricks’ acquisition of Neon further validate the market perception of Postgres as a strategic anchor for transactional and AI workloads.

catalysts.catalyst_details: MongoDB maintains its position as the 5th most popular database overall (DB-Engines) and the leading document database, with steady developer usage in Stack Overflow surveys.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: MongoDB shows up as the 5th most popular database overall (DB-Engines) and the leading document database (Exhibit 8), with developer usage steady in the mid-20’s in recent Stack Overflow survey’s, not collapsing.

catalysts.catalyst_details: MongoDB was recognized as AWS’s North America Partner of the Year in 2024 and Microsoft’s in 2025, highlighting strong co-sell and marketplace momentum.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Co-sell and marketplace momentum underscore this trajectory - MongoDB was AWS’s North America Partner of the Year in 2024 and Microsoft’s in 2025

catalysts.catalyst_details: The SSPL license, introduced in 2018, continues to discourage direct reselling of MongoDB's latest code, protecting its intellectual property and competitive position.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: and the SSPL license (2018) continues to discourage direct reselling of the latest code.

catalysts.catalyst_details: MongoDB acquired Voyage AI in February 2025 to integrate its embedding and reranking models, enhancing Atlas's native AI capabilities.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: The Feb. 2025 Voyage AI acquisition tightens this loop.

catalysts.catalyst_details: MongoDB shows strong adoption metrics for AI, with roughly 8,000 startups building on the platform (as of Aug 2025), 200K new developer registrations monthly, and 30% of Atlas ARR from AI use cases.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: MongoDB has roughly 8,000 startups building on the platform (as of Aug. 2025), 200K new developer registrations monthly and 30% of Atlas ARR is generated from customers with at least one AI use case.

catalysts.catalyst_details: MongoDB is expanding its partner set with major AI ecosystem players (LangChain, Galileo, Temporal, Vercel, Anthropic, Cohere) to help customers shorten time-to-production for AI applications.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: MongoDB’s broader emphasis on expanding its partner set with major ecosystem players – LangChain, Galileo, Temporal, Vercel, Anthropic, Cohere – helping customers shorten time-to-production by pairing the platform with critical tools and services in the AI value chain.

catalysts.catalyst_details: AI workloads are expected to be a growing (albeit modest) contributor to Atlas consumption as the company progresses through FY27.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: With Voyage now embedded natively in the developer data platform, along with Vector and Hybrid Search, we believe MongoDB is positioned well from a product perspective; combined with an expanding list of AI startups and ongoing investments in developer awareness, we believe AI workloads can be a growing (albeit modest) contributor to Atlas consumption as we progress through FY27.

catalysts.catalyst_details: MongoDB has explicitly shifted its Go-to-Market (GTM) focus upmarket, concentrating sales resources on larger enterprises and strategic workloads, while leveraging self-serve and partners for smaller accounts.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Over the last year, MongoDB has explicitly shifted its GTM focus upmarket, concentrating sales resources on larger enterprises and more strategic workloads, while allowing smaller, transactional activity to run through self-serve and partners.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Self-serve net customer additions increased by +93% from F1Q-F3Q26 compared to the same period in FY25, indicating a robust self-serve channel.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: At the same time, the company continues to benefit from a robust self-serve channel that feeds high-quality top-of-funnel activity; self-serve net customer adds are up +93% from F1Q-F3Q26 vs. the same period in FY25.

catalysts.catalyst_details: 25% of customers with >$1M ARR were sourced through the self-serve channel and scaled to $1M+ 15% faster than those from other channels.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Note that 25% of >$1M ARR Customers were sourced through the self-serve channel and scaled to $1M+ 15% faster than those that came through other channels.

catalysts.catalyst_details: 70% of Atlas ARR is driven by customers adopting at least one additional capability beyond the core database, deepening platform dependence.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: 70% of Atlas ARR is driven by customers adopting at least one additional capability.

catalysts.catalyst_details: MongoDB's growing focus on legacy modernization through programs like AMP creates a long-tailed opportunity to re-platform existing applications.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Meanwhile, MongoDB’s growing focus on legacy modernization through programs like AMP – targeting entrenched relational estates – creates a long-tailed opportunity to re-platform existing applications, not just net-new ones.

catalysts.catalyst_details: MongoDB raised its FY26 Operating Margin guidance from an initial 10% to 18% (F3Q26), largely due to revenue outperformance.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: FY26 is representative: the company initially provided an outlook for 10% OpM but subsequently raised guidance to 18% (F3Q26), largely on Revenue outperformance from both Atlas and non-Atlas.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Reallocation of S&M resources upmarket and pivot to a scaled self-serve motion are expected to translate to higher incremental S&M efficiency over the mid-term.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: As is typical with most software companies, we believe S&M will be the primary source of leverage over the mid-term. MongoDB’s reallocation of resources upmarket, where rep productivity is historically high, coupled with its pivot towards a scaled and effective self-serve motion for down-market new logo acquisition, should translate to higher incremental S&M efficiency over time.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Longer-term, as Atlas scales, growth is expected to be increasingly fueled by new workload expansion and organic workload growth, reducing reliance on proportional sales hiring.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: And longer term, as Atlas scales, we would expect a lower reliance on proportional sales hiring with growth increasingly fueled by new workload expansion and organic workload growth, rather than new logo acquisition.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Increased competitive pressure from relational database incumbents, particularly from Hyperscalers like Oracle, Microsoft, AWS, and GCP, poses a downside risk.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Key downside risks include: 1) Increased competitive pressure from relational database incumbents, particularly from Hyperscalers like Oracle, Microsoft, AWS, and GCP

catalysts.catalyst_details: Broader standardization around Postgres could negatively impact MongoDB’s win rates and expansion opportunities.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: 2) Broader standardization around Postgres impacts MongoDB’s win rates and expansion opportunities

catalysts.catalyst_details: A slowing pace in cloud migration, multi-cloud adoption, digital transformation, and application modernization poses a downside risk.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: 3) Slowing pace in cloud migration, multi-cloud adoption, digital transformation, and application modernization

catalysts.catalyst_details: The AI cycle proving to be a less meaningful tailwind than initially contemplated is a downside risk.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: 4) AI cycle proves to be a less meaningful tailwind than initially contemplated

catalysts.catalyst_details: Potential margin degradation due to required reinvestment or unanticipated headwinds from the Enterprise Agreement (EA) business is a downside risk.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: 5) Margin degradation due to required reinvestment or unanticipated headwinds from the EA business

catalysts.catalyst_details: Adverse changes in the overall IT spending environment pose a downside risk.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: 6) Adverse changes in the IT spending environment.

bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: The platform now spans full-text search, vector search, stream processing, data federation, time-series, and robust security (including queryable encryption), collapsing moving parts and speeding time-to-value.

bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: While MongoDB’s revenue scale is now $2.5B+, its market share remains roughly 2%, leaving substantial runway as applications modernize, buyers standardize on managed services, and AI becomes embedded in software.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Atlas has compounded at over 60% from FY19-26E, validating product-market fit and customer trust.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Atlas has compounded at over 60% from FY19-26E to more than $1.8B in run-rate Revenue despite native cloud and open-source alternatives, validating product–market fit and customer trust.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Combination of developer velocity, enterprise-grade scale, multi-cloud neutrality, and usage-based pricing underpins durable growth.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We believe the combination of developer velocity and enterprise-grade scale, delivered with multi-cloud neutrality and usage-based pricing, underpins durable growth and rising platform relevance.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: MongoDB’s architecture is increasingly aligned with modern software development, offering flexibility and scalability.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: MongoDB’s architecture increasingly aligned with the direction of modern software development. MongoDB’s core design allows developers to store complex, real-world data structures in a single flexible format and scale applications across many severs as traffic grows, making it well-suited for modern, fast-changing workloads.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Reduced friction for product evolution due to flexible data model, avoiding constant schema redesigns.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: This flexibility reduces friction when products evolve, because teams can adjust how data is captured and used without constantly redesigning a rigid schema.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Closing historical gaps against relational alternatives with stronger consistency, better performance, and richer indexing.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Importantly, MongoDB has spent the last several product cycles closing gaps that historically pushed teams toward relational alternatives: stronger consistency guarantees, better performance, richer indexing and more predictable scaling behavior.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Version 8.0 delivered considerable performance gains (36% faster reads, 59% higher update throughput).
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: In Version 8.0, MongoDB delivered considerable performance gains – 36% faster reads and 59% higher update throughput

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Atlas acts as a hub for adjacent capabilities like full-text search, vector search, stream processing, and analytics.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: On top of the core database, Atlas now acts as a hub for adjacent capabilities: full-text search, vector search, stream processing and lightweight analytics all run in the same environment.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Consolidation of capabilities improves developer productivity, lowers operational complexity, and deepens reliance on MongoDB.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We see this consolidation improving developer productivity, lowering operational complexity and ultimately deepening reliance on MongoDB as the application’s data layer rather than just another database.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: MongoDB is gaining market share in its specific use cases, not primarily at the expense of Postgres.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We see Postgres and MongoDB both gaining share in their respective lanes, not primarily from each other.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Architecturally better suited for horizontal scalability compared to Postgres.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: It is also architecturally better suited for horizontal scalability: MongoDB is designed from the ground up to distribute data and load across many servers, while scaling Postgres to similar levels requires additional tooling and engineering effort.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Provides a simpler, more integrated path for AI-enabled applications with flexible data model, built-in search, and vector capabilities.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: As AI-enabled applications increasingly mix user context, unstructured content and model embeddings, MongoDB’s combination of a flexible data model with built-in search and vector capabilities often provides a simpler, more integrated path than layering JSON and AI extensions onto a relational core.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Current performance metrics (Atlas growth, new logos, expansion rates) are inconsistent with competitive displacement from Postgres.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: If Postgres were truly emerging as a material headwind to MongoDB, we would not be seeing it in the numbers – and so far, we see the opposite. Atlas growth has accelerated for three consecutive quarters (+30% in F3Q26, Exhibit 5), Atlas new logo adds are at record highs (2500+ vs. 1800 average from FY23-25, Exhibit 4), and Net Expansion Rates are stable at 120%, all of which are inconsistent with a story of mounting competitive displacement.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Continues to win net-new and modernization workloads valuing flexible data models and horizontal scalability, especially for AI use cases.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: In our view, the data supports a world where Postgres is successfully replacing older relational systems, while MongoDB continues to win net-new and modernization workloads that value flexible data models and horizontal scalability, particularly as AI use cases become more prominent.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Successfully competing and winning against hyperscalers for years.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Crucially, though, MongoDB has been competing and winning in this environment for years.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Sustained share gains among pure-plays and consistent/growing slice of net new spend.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Path for share expansion as the company moves upmarket and standardizes in larger enterprises.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: we see a path for that share to expand as the company moves upmarket and standardizes in larger enterprises.

bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Public benchmarks point to a meaningful capability and adoption edge versus most look-alike services, which can lag on key features like Queryable Encryption and Vector Search.

thesis_analysis.central_thesis: MongoDB is positioned as a foundational, independent, general-purpose data platform capable of significant growth due to enterprise application modernization and the embedding of AI at scale, driven by its flexible document model, strong developer affinity, and the rapidly growing Atlas service, which integrates search, streaming, and vector capabilities to reduce complexity for AI applications and enable durable mid-20's Atlas growth with improving profitability.

sub_claims.claim: MongoDB demonstrates strong financial performance, including revenue outperformance, accelerated Atlas revenue growth, and improved profitability metrics, indicating a transition to durable and profitable high-growth at scale.

evidence_list.credibility_assessment: Moderate, as it's an analyst's recommendation and target.

evidence_list.content: Revenue was +5.8% above Consensus.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High, as it's a quantitative financial result compared to market expectations.

evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High, as it's a quantitative growth metric.

evidence_list.content: Non-Atlas outperformed largely on higher multi-year deals.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High, as it's a reported driver of financial performance.

evidence_list.content: OpM / FCFM came in +800 bps / + 1800 bps ahead on Revenue outperformance and higher cash collections.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High, as it's a quantitative measure of margin outperformance.

evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High, as it's a quantitative performance metric.

evidence_list.content: Net ARR Expansion Rate improved to 120% from 119% in the prior Q.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High, as it's a quantitative customer expansion metric.

evidence_list.content: Management reaffirmed its framework to deliver +100-200 bps of OpM expansion in FY27 and beyond.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High, as it's a direct statement of management's financial guidance.

evidence_list.content: FY26 is representative: the company initially provided an outlook for 10% OpM but subsequently raised guidance to 18% (F3Q26), largely on Revenue outperformance from both Atlas and non-Atlas.

evidence_list.content: Upside: 44.4%.
